rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY/LAW & MOTION CALENDAR for Wednesday, May-28-2025, Line 1 PLAINTIFF GREATER CALIFORNIA FINANCIAL COMPANY, LLC, ASSIGNEE OF CHASE MANHATTAN BANK HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION Before the court is Greater California Financial Company, LLC's (assignee of Plaintiff and Judgment Creditor Chase Manhattan Bank) Application to Determine Defendant and Judgment Debtor Roya Mohareri's Claim of Exemption (Wage Garnishment). Hearing required. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: ST. PAULUS EVANGELICAL LUTHERAN CHURCH, A VS. MERKER ARCHITECTURAL GROUP INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY/LAW & MOTION CALENDAR for Wednesday, May-28-2025, Line DEFENDANT MERKER ARCHITECTURAL GROUP INC., MARC MERKER MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD Before the court is a Motion to be relieved as Counsel from David A. Erickson and Ericksen Legal, PC, counsel for Defendants Marc Dana Merker and Merker Architectural Group, Inc. The request is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. Current counsel of record for Defendants Marc Dana Merker and Merker Architectural Group, Inc. are David A. Erickson and Murtaugh Treglia Stem & Deily LLP ("The Murtaugh Firm"). Moving Parties failed to timely serve their request to withdraw on The Murtaugh Firm, Such service is necessary. Indeed, if this request were granted, it would authorize David Ericksen's withdrawal, but not The Murtaugh Firm's, who would still be counsel of record. It is apparent from the motion that this is not the intended or desired outcome. The court will not move forward on any request to be relieved as counsel for Defendants absent at the very least, timely notice to the Murtaugh Firm. Moreover, the court will not relieve the Murtaugh Firm absent a proper request from the firm. Notably, too, the current record does not provide a basis to authorize withdraw by the Murtaugh Firm; not currently having a member with knowledge of the case is not grounds, nor is no longer having an interest in representing the parties. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: MATHILDE FROUSTEY VS. SAN FRANCISCO BALLET ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Deposition In Compliance With Code Of Civil Procedure Section 2025.290(B)(4), And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY/LAW & MOTION CALENDAR for Wednesday, May-28-2025, Line 11, 2 - DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO BALLET ASSOCIATION Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Deposition In Compliance With Code Of Civil Procedure Section 2025.290(B)(4), And For Sanctions Defendant San Francisco Ballet Association's motion to compel Plaintiff's deposition in compliance with CCP 2025.290(b)(4) is DENIED. This motion is about the duration of a deposition that has not yet commenced. "Except as provided in subdivision (b), or by any court order, including a case management order, a deposition examination of the witness by all counsel, other than the witness' counsel of record, shall be limited to seven hours of total testimony. The court shall allow additional time, beyond any limits imposed by this section, if needed to fairly examine the deponent or if the deponent, another person, or any other circumstance impedes or delays the examination.) (Code of Civil Procedure section 2025.290(a).) "This section shall not apply under any of the following circumstances:  (4) [t]o any case brought by an employee or applicant for employment against an employer for acts or omissions arising out of or relating to the employment relationship." (Code of Civil Procedure section 2025.290(b)(4).) Plaintiff has agreed to a seven-hour deposition over Zoom on May 29, 2025. If, after seven hours, Defendant believes more time is needed and Plaintiff disagrees, Defendant may move to compel further deposition after meeting and conferring on the issue. The court express no view on whether CCP 2025.290(b)(4) applies. The court finds that Defendant did not act with substantial justification in connection with this discovery. Defendant San Francisco Ballet Association and its counsel, jointly and severally, shall pay $1,500 as sanctions, payable to the Clerk of the Court, payment no later than by June 16, 2025, receipt filed no later than June 23, 2025. The court admonishes the parties that further discovery motions may result in a discovery referee referral. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: PAUL HENRY VS. ERIC YOUNG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Defendant Emmanuel Kampouris'S Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Defendant Andrew Smith Lewis'S Motion For Sanctions Under Ccp Section 128.7
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY/LAW & MOTION CALENDAR for Wednesday, May-28-2025, Line 6, 1 - PLAINTIFF PAUL HENRY'S Motion To Strike Defendant Emmanuel Kampouris'S Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Defendant Andrew Smith Lewis'S Motion For Sanctions Under Ccp Section 128.7 Plaintiff Paul Henry's Motion To Strike Defendant Emmanuel Kampouris's Memorandum of Points and Authorities in Support of Defendant Andrew Smith Lewis's Motion For Sanctions Under CCP Section 128.7 is DENIED. Plaintiff movers under Code of Civil Procedure section 435. "Any party, within the time allowed to respond to a pleading may serve and file a notice of motion to strike the whole or any part thereof" (CCP 435.) "The court may, upon a motion made pursuant to Section 435, or at any time in its discretion, and upon terms it deems proper: [para] (a) Strike out any irrelevant, false, or improper matter inserted in any pleading. (b) Strike out all or any part of any pleading not drawn or filed in conformity with the laws of this state, a court rule, or an order of the court." (CCP 436.) Here, Plaintiff has failed to demonstrate Defendant Kampouris's memorandum is a "pleading." Nor had Plaintiff demonstrated the memorandum is prohibited under Code of Civil Procedure section 128.7. Additionally, the motion that the memorandum supported has been decided. Even if there were a basis to strike the memorandum, the court would not exercise its discretion under section 435 in favor of striking it. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. rulings.Case Title: NAUMAN NADEEM VS. PARADIGM HOTELS GROUP LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Statutory Attorneys Fees And Costs Pursuant To Cal. Code Of Civil Procedure 1281.98(C) And 1281.99(A)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY/LAW & MOTION CALENDAR for Wednesday, May-28-2025, Line 7 PLAINTIFF NAUMAN NADEEM's Motion For Statutory Attorneys Fees And Costs Pursuant To Cal. Code Of Civil Procedure 1281.98(C) And 1281.99(A) Plaintiff Nauman Nadeem's unopposed Motion For Statutory Attorney's Fees and Costs Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure Sections 1281.98(c) and 1281.99(a) is GRANTED. The court finds the record supports an award of fees under Code of Civil Procedure sections 1281.98(c) and 1281.99(a). For purposes of this motion only, the billable hourly rates of Plaintiff's counsel are reasonable and not inconsistent with attorneys of similar experience for similar work in the relevant legal market. The hours expended by Plaintiffs' counsel are found to be reasonable and limited to those hours that were spent in connection with the abandoned arbitration matter and as a result of Defendants' material breach of the arbitration agreement. The Court finds the costs expended in the sum of $484.40 to be reasonable, necessary, recoverable, and limited to only those costs that were incurred in connection with the abandoned arbitration matter and as a result of Defendants' material breach of the arbitration agreement. Accordingly, the Court awards to Plaintiff his attorneys' fees in the total sum of $12,900.00, as well as his costs in the total sum of $484.40, for a total award of attorneys' fees and costs of $13,384.40, payable to JML Law Client Trust Account as payment of Plaintiff's attorneys' fees and costs. Defendants Paradigm Hotels Group LLC and 250 Fourth Development, LP are jointly and severally liable for payment of the $13,384.40 in attorneys' fees and costs to Plaintiff, which shall be paid within 60 days of notice of entry of this Order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. rulings.Case Title: DON LEMON VS. ELON MUSK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For (1) Specified Discovery Under Ccp 425.16, And (2) A Brief Continuance Of The Hearing Date On X Corp.'S Anti-Slap Motion, Defendants Demurrer, And Related Deadlines; Declaration Of Bryan Kirsh; Exhibits
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY/LAW & MOTION CALENDAR for Wednesday, May-28-2025, Line 8 4 - PLAINTIFF DON LEMON'S Motion For (1) Specified Discovery Under Ccp 425.16, And (2) A Brief Continuance Of The Hearing Date On X Corp.'S Anti-Slap Motion, Defendants Demurrer, And Related Deadlines Continued to June 9, 2025, on the court's own motion. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
